updates.title: Self-update
updates.available_message: Version {{latest}} is ready to install (currently running {{current}}).
updates.confirm_install: Install version {{version}}? The container will briefly restart. Your data is preserved.
updates.up_to_date: You're running the latest stable release.
updates.channel_hint: Which releases are eligible to install.
updates.mode_hint: What happens when an update is available.

login.title: Sign in
login.unknown_error: Something went wrong. Try again in a moment.
login.restore_hint: Have a backup from a previous CloudGate install?

onboarding.title: Welcome to CloudGate
onboarding.step5_intro: Quick health-check of every CloudGate subsystem. This shows you whether the install is wired up correctly before we let you loose.
onboarding.verify_db_failed_hint: DB locked or missing — try restarting the container.
onboarding.verify_secrets_failed_hint: encryption.key or jwt.key missing — bootstrap incomplete, check container logs.
onboarding.verify_cloudflared_failed_hint: Daemon not running — normal until you create your first tunnel.
onboarding.verify_disk_failed_hint: Less than 100 MB free on /data — clean up logs or extend the volume.
onboarding.verify_github_failed_hint: Can't reach api.github.com — updater won't work but the rest of the app does.
onboarding.verify_some_failed_body: That's not always a problem — for example, cloudflared is idle until you create a tunnel. You can continue and fix things later from Settings.
onboarding.tour_hint: A short walkthrough of every CloudGate page starts after you click. You can skip it at any time.
onboarding.welcome_body: Three short steps to expose your first service through Cloudflare Tunnel:
onboarding.welcome_bullet2: Create a tunnel — your bridge to Cloudflare's edge
onboarding.welcome_bullet3: Add a host: hostname + local IP+port → live in ~30 seconds
onboarding.step2_intro: CloudGate needs API access to manage tunnels and DNS records on your behalf. You stay in full control — we never use any other permissions.
onboarding.step2_already_have: You already have a Cloudflare account configured. Continue to the next step.
onboarding.cf_step1: Sign in to dash.cloudflare.com → My Profile → API Tokens → Create Token → Custom Token
onboarding.step3_intro: A tunnel is a single secure outbound connection from CloudGate to Cloudflare. One tunnel can host many websites — name it whatever you'd like.
onboarding.step3_already_have: Tunnel already exists. Continue to add your first host.
onboarding.need_account_first: Add a Cloudflare account in the previous step first.
onboarding.step4_intro: Map a hostname (like immich.yourdomain.com) to a local service (like 192.168.1.10:8080). CloudGate creates the DNS record and pipes traffic through the tunnel automatically.
onboarding.done_body: You're all set. A few pointers:
onboarding.done_hint_dashboard: Live overview of tunnels and hosts

tour.stop_1_body: Quick guided tour — about a minute. We'll walk through every page and show you what's where. Press Skip any time to dismiss.
tour.stop_2_body: These cards show your Cloudflare accounts, active tunnels, live hosts and the running version. They auto-refresh every 10 seconds.
tour.stop_3_body: All sections of CloudGate live here. Cloudflare for tokens, Tunnels for the daemon, Hosts for your services. Settings has your profile, 2FA and backups.
tour.stop_4_body: Pick 'Add host' to expose a single service. Pick 'Bulk import' to paste a CSV with many at once — perfect for migrating from another tool.
tour.stop_5_body: Each host can either go through a Cloudflare Tunnel (default, works behind CGNAT) or through a local nginx reverse-proxy (when you DO have a public IP and want raw speed).
tour.stop_6_body: Each tunnel shows live daemon status. Click the terminal icon to tail the last 200 log lines — super useful for diagnosing connectivity hiccups.
tour.stop_7_body: You can connect multiple Cloudflare accounts here. Each has its own set of zones (domains) and tunnels. Tokens are stored encrypted with AES-256-GCM.
tour.stop_8_body: Set up TOTP with any authenticator app (Aegis, Authy, 1Password). Strongly recommended for any internet-exposed install.
tour.stop_9_body: Download a single .cgbk file containing your DB, secrets and tunnel credentials. AES-256-GCM encrypted with your passphrase — store it safely.
tour.stop_10_body: Every action that changes state (login, host create, tunnel restart, settings change) is recorded here with timestamp + IP. Filter to investigate.
tour.stop_11_body: CloudGate self-updates by polling GitHub releases every 6 hours. Choose your channel and whether to install automatically or only notify.
tour.stop_12_body: CloudGate is MIT licensed and maintained by one person. If it saves you a Sunday afternoon, a small tip is hugely appreciated — but starring on GitHub helps just as much.

restore.title: Restore from backup
restore.intro: Upload an encrypted .cgbk file from a previous CloudGate install. This only works on a fresh container — your existing data is protected.
restore.note: After restore, the container needs to restart (a single 'docker restart cloudgate' or simply restart the host).
restore.success_body: {{files}} files restored ({{mb}} MB total). Restart the container, then login with your previous credentials.

password.title: Change password
password.force_message: Your current password was auto-generated. Please choose a new one before continuing.
password.hint: At least 12 characters. Choose something you haven't used elsewhere.
password.first_setup_alert_body: Pick your own email + name and change the password. This is the only time you'll see this screen.
password.first_setup_done_message: Account set up. Let's add your first Cloudflare token.
password.account_email_hint: Used for login and notifications. Defaults to admin@cloudgate.local.
password.email_taken: That email is already used by another user.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.placeholder_hint: Browse Cloudflare, Tunnels and Hosts in the sidebar.
dashboard.hosts_errors_message: {{count}} host(s) failed to deploy. Open the Hosts page and hover the red status badge for details.
dashboard.next_step_message: You have a Cloudflare account configured.

settings.title: Settings
settings.totp_hint: Add a second login step using an authenticator app like Aegis, Authy or 1Password.
settings.totp_scan_qr: Scan the QR code with your authenticator app, then enter the 6-digit code:
settings.totp_enabled_message: From now on, login requires your authenticator code.
settings.totp_disable_warning: Your account will be less secure. Confirm with your password.
settings.backup_hint: Exports your DB + secrets + tunnel credentials as a single encrypted file. Move to a new machine by importing during first-run setup (coming).
settings.backup_modal_hint: The passphrase encrypts the backup file. You'll need it to restore — store it somewhere safe, separately from the backup itself.
settings.support_body: CloudGate is free and open source. If it saves you time, a one-time tip helps cover infrastructure + signing keys + the time spent fixing bugs.
settings.help_body: Re-run the onboarding wizard or replay the guided app-tour at any time. Both are safe to run repeatedly — they don't change any of your data.

donate.title: Support CloudGate
donate.intro: CloudGate is free and open source. If it saves you a Sunday afternoon, a small one-time tip is hugely appreciated — it covers GHCR storage, signing certs, and the time spent on bug-fixes + new features.
donate.thanks: Every contribution is read personally. Thank you! 🙏
donate.paypal_hint: Opens in a new tab. PayPal account, card or PayPal Credit.
donate.eth_hint: Mainnet · scan with any wallet (EIP-681 URI).
donate.sol_hint: Mainnet · scan with Phantom, Solflare, Backpack…
donate.alternative_body: No problem — there are other ways to help:
donate.report_bug: Report a bug or feature request

audit.title: Audit log
audit.description: All sensitive operations (login, 2FA changes, backups, host changes) are recorded here.
audit.empty: No audit entries yet. Activities will appear as you use CloudGate.

cloudflare.title: Cloudflare accounts
cloudflare.hint: Add your Cloudflare API token to let CloudGate create tunnels and DNS records on your behalf.
cloudflare.add_hint: Paste an API token with the scopes Tunnels:Edit, DNS:Edit and Zone:Read.
cloudflare.token_too_short: Token looks too short — paste the full token from Cloudflare.
cloudflare.empty: No Cloudflare accounts yet. Add one to get started.
cloudflare.confirm_delete: Delete {{label}}? This removes the account and all cached zones.
cloudflare.no_zones: No zones cached. Click refresh to fetch them.

tunnels.title: Cloudflare tunnels
tunnels.empty: No tunnels yet. Create one to expose services.
tunnels.name_hint: Letters, numbers, dashes and underscores only.
tunnels.invalid_name: Use letters, numbers, dashes and underscores only.
tunnels.confirm_delete: Delete tunnel {{name}}? It will also be removed from Cloudflare.

bulk.title: Bulk import hosts
bulk.intro: Upload or paste a CSV with one host per row. Header row is optional. Available columns: hostname, forward_host, forward_port, forward_scheme, mode, tunnel_id, cf_zone_id, no_tls_verify.
bulk.default_tunnel: Default tunnel for rows without one
bulk.default_tunnel_hint: Used as fallback when a row omits tunnel_id.
bulk.default_zone: Default zone for rows without one

hosts.title: Hosts
hosts.empty: No hosts yet. Add one to expose a local service.
hosts.no_tls_verify_hint: Tick this if your local service uses a self-signed cert.
hosts.no_cf_warning: Add a Cloudflare account first before creating hosts.
hosts.no_tunnel_warning: Create a tunnel first under Tunnels.
hosts.invalid_hostname: Enter a valid domain name (e.g. immich.example.com).
hosts.confirm_delete: Delete host {{hostname}}? DNS record will also be removed.
hosts.errors_present_body: Hover the red status badge for details. Often a missing token scope:
hosts.confirm_issue_cert: Acquire a Let's Encrypt certificate for {{hostname}} via DNS-01? CloudGate will create a temporary TXT record in Cloudflare and verify, then save the cert. Takes ~1 minute.
